response:
Mode: create. Readiness: Stage 2 (model-grounded). Eligibility: eligible. All six gates pass when the operator chooses only zone slot limits at the start of the window; regular capacity, forecasts, margins, overtime rates, and slot bounds are decision-time inputs; arrivals and service times remain uncertain; accepted load generates capacity use and overtime; constraints retain regular courier-hours and zone bounds; and the objective stays expected same-day margin minus overtime cost.

Business framing: At the service-window cutoff, an operator sets how many same-day order slots to expose in each zone. More slots can admit margin-bearing orders but can load scarce courier-hours and create overtime when spatial demand or service times are high. Tighter limits protect capacity and overtime expense but can forgo expected margin. The managerial question is how to distribute advance slot availability across zones under uncertain arrivals and service times. A bounded research question is: Which zone slot limits maximize expected same-day margin net of overtime within regular capacity and zone bounds? The model supports no retention or lifetime-value claim, and scholarly novelty is unverified.

Model mapping: zone slot limits = start-of-window decisions; regular capacity, forecasts, margins, and overtime rates = decision inputs; arrivals and service time = later uncertainty; realized orders and overtime = outcomes; courier-hour capacity and zone bounds = constraints; expected same-day margin minus overtime cost = objective. Slots and courier-hours are distinct units; detailed horizon and currency are unspecified.

Evidence and boundaries: 'same-day promises improve loyalty' is unsupported and must be removed or tested with customer-level evidence and an extended objective. No supplied evidence validates forecasts, capacity use, or realized impact. Counterconditions include lifetime-value effects, dynamic slot adjustment, or capacity recourse not represented here. Primary remaining weakness: operational calibration and the practical magnitude of the trade-off are unsupported.
